message:
refactor(mcp): extract framework-coupled logic from mcp_client into mcp_framework seam

Strangler-fig migration step: moves all fastmcp-specific conversion logic
out of the public MCP client module and into the framework boundary layer,
so the outer module deals only with FrankenTerm-native types.

Extractions from mcp_client.rs → mcp_framework.rs:
- McpClientToolDefinition::from_framework / into_framework
- McpClientContentItem::from_framework / into_framework
- ExternalServerConfig::from_discovered (inlined into discover fn)
- build_loader() config loader construction
- roundtrip_framework_payload() and framework_payload_error() helpers

New framework-side abstractions:
- OutboundFrameworkClient: wraps FrameworkClient, exposes
  list_tool_definitions() and call_tool_content() that return
  McpClient-native types directly
- OutboundFrameworkError: distinguishes Transport (framework) vs Mapping
  (serialization) errors so callers can map them appropriately
- DiscoveredFrameworkServers: bundles search_paths + sorted server list
- discover_server_configs(): encapsulates loader construction + discovery

mcp_client.rs simplifications:
- FtMcpClient::connect() delegates builder config to
  OutboundFrameworkClient::connect_stdio()
- list_tools() and call_tool() no longer perform manual from_framework
  mapping — that's handled inside the outbound client
- Error handling uses OutboundFrameworkError match arms
- Removed unused Display import

Tests added in mcp_framework.rs:
- tool_definition_roundtrips_across_framework_seam
- tool_definition_rejects_invalid_icon_payload
- content_item_roundtrips_across_framework_seam

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
